title: astragalus subinduratus
intro: Astragalus subinduratus, also known as the hard-seeded milk vetch, is a type of perennial legume plant native to China and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Astragalus subinduratus include improved immune function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced cardiovascular health, making it a popular natural remedy for conditions such as fatigue, anxiety, and cardiovascular disease. Astragalus subinduratus can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
